title: Reconnecting with Nature: A Step in the Right Direction
subtitle: 人类与自然的疏离是福是祸

paragraphs.en: The weakening of the human connection to nature might be good for economic growth but is bad for people. A tipping point was reached in 2020 when human-made materials — such as steel, concrete and plastic — were found to weigh more than all life on Earth. Continuing to grow concrete forests rather than real ones is shortsighted. Simply being in the nearest wood has such health benefits that the Woodland Trust successfully lobbied for it to be prescribed by doctors. Yet slipping from popular culture is the wonder and beauty of the natural world.
paragraphs.zh: 人类与自然联系的减弱或许有利于经济增长，但对人类自身却是不利的。2020年达到一个临界点，当时发现钢铁、混凝土和塑料等人造材料的总重量已超过地球上所有生物的总和。继续培植混凝土森林而非真正的森林是短视的。仅是在最近的树林中待一会儿便有显著健康益处，以至于林地信托基金成功游说医生将其作为处方开具。然而，自然界的奇妙与美丽正在从流行文化中悄然消失。

paragraphs.en: For every three nature-related words in hit songs of the 1950s, researchers found, there was only slightly more than one 50 years later. It is not a moment too soon that teenagers will be able to take a natural history test, given that for decades children have been able to name more video game characters than wildlife species. Part of remedying this social disease would be for parliament to pass a right to grow law, allowing anyone to turn underused public spaces into vegetable and fruit gardens. The idea is for people to get back in touch with the soil — while producing food sustainably.
paragraphs.zh: 研究人员发现，20世纪50年代热门歌曲中每出现三个与自然相关的词汇，50年后仅剩略多于一个。考虑到几十年来孩子们能叫出名字的电子游戏角色比野生动物种类还多，青少年能够参加自然史考试一事可谓刻不容缓。治愈这一社会痼疾的部分良方是议会通过一项“种植权”法案，允许任何人将闲置公共空间变为蔬果园。其理念是让人们重新接触土壤，同时可持续地生产食物。

paragraphs.en: Vegetable planting has a respectable tradition. In April 1649, locals responded to high prices and food shortages by cultivating vegetables on common land in Southern England. The practice of throwing seed bombs to turn vacant plots of land green took off in 1970s New York, and has been revived by green-thumbed social media influencers who defy local U.S. regulations in a war on ugly spots in cities.
paragraphs.zh: 蔬菜种植有着体面的传统。1649年4月，当地民众为应对高物价和食物短缺，在英格兰南部的公共土地上种植蔬菜。投掷种子炸弹将空地变绿的做法于20世纪70年代在纽约兴起，并被拥有园艺天赋的社交媒体网红重新发扬光大，他们无视美国当地法规，向城市中的丑陋角落宣战。

paragraphs.en: Apart from the urgent task of providing more healthy nutrients to those who increasingly can't afford them, publicly accessible fruit and vegetable gardens connect what we eat to where it comes from — the means of production, if you will. They can make unlovely spaces lovely, and marry use and beauty as well as help promote a sense of community. Plants are also, of course, our first defence against species loss and climate change. Such planting is a small step for humanity — in the right direction.
paragraphs.zh: 除了为越来越多买不起健康食品的人提供营养这一紧迫任务外，公共蔬果园还将我们所吃之物与其来源——不妨称之为生产资料——联系起来。它们能让丑陋的空间变得可爱，将实用与美观相结合，并有助于促进社区意识。当然，植物也是我们抵御物种灭绝和气候变化的第一道防线。这样的种植是人类迈出的一小步，却是朝着正确方向的一步。

words.meaning: adj. 可进入的，可使用的
